quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Genetics: Advanced Concepts and Inheritance Patterns
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Polymorphism, Genetic Variation, Impossible F₁ Outcomes, Blood Type Inheritance, Sex Determination, Acquired vs Inherited Traits, Probability Calculations

questions.question: Long radishes crossed with round radishes result in all oval radishes. This type of inheritance is:
questions.explanation: Oval radishes from long × round show incomplete dominance, where heterozygotes display an intermediate phenotype. Complete dominance would show either long or round, not intermediate.

questions.question: If one parent has type A blood and the other parent has type B blood, what blood type will the offspring have?
questions.explanation: Depending on the parents' genotypes, offspring can have A, B, AB, or O blood types. For example, if parents are Iᴬi and Iᴮi, they can produce children with A (Iᴬi), B (Iᴮi), AB (IᴬIᴮ), or O (ii). All four blood types are possible.

questions.question: The occurrence of two or more alleles of a gene in a population is called:
questions.explanation: Genetic polymorphism refers to the presence of two or more distinct alleles of a gene in a population. Homozygosity and heterozygosity refer to individual genotypes, not population-level variation.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a mechanism of genetic variation?
questions.explanation: Genetic drift is a change in allele frequencies due to chance events, not a mechanism that creates new variation. Mutation creates new alleles, sexual reproduction (crossing over, independent assortment) shuffles existing variation, and gene flow introduces alleles from other populations.

questions.question: Select all that apply: In Mendel's experiment crossing a purebred purple-flowered plant (PP) with a purebred white-flowered plant (pp), which of the following results would be physically impossible in the first generation of offspring (F₁)?
options: A mix of 3 purple-flowered and 1 white-flowered plant
questions.explanation: PP × pp produces all Pp in F₁, which are all purple (complete dominance). So 100% purple is expected. A mix of purple and white (B) is impossible in F₁ (that's F₂). Lavender plants (D) would require incomplete dominance, which pea flowers don't show.

questions.question: Which of the following is a totally impossible outcome of Mendel's Experiment (cross breeding pure bred tall and short pea plants)?
questions.explanation: Tall (TT) × short (tt) produces all tall (Tt) in F₁. Medium-height plants are impossible because pea plant height shows complete dominance, not incomplete dominance. The other ratios are possible in F₂ (3:1) or F₁ (all tall).

questions.question: Which one of the following is not one of the direct conclusions that can be drawn from Mendel's experiment?
options: Only one parental trait is expressed
Two copies of each trait is inherited in sexually reproducing organism
For recessive trait to be expressed, both copies should be identical
Natural selection can alter frequency of an inherited trait
questions.explanation: Mendel's experiments established principles of inheritance (dominance, segregation, independent assortment). Natural selection altering trait frequencies is an evolutionary concept from Darwin, not a direct conclusion from Mendel's breeding experiments.

questions.question: Select all that apply: In a cross between a pure-bred tall plant with round seeds (TTRR) and a short plant with wrinkled seeds (ttrr), which of the following phenotypes can appear in the F₂ generation?
questions.explanation: TTRR × ttrr produces all TtRr in F₁. F₂ from TtRr × TtRr produces all four combinations in 9:3:3:1 ratio: tall-round, tall-wrinkled, short-round, short-wrinkled. Medium-height or oval seeds are not possible (complete dominance, not incomplete).

questions.question: Select all that apply: Which of the following are possible progeny in the F₂ generation when a pure-bred tall plant with round seeds is crossed with a pure-bred short plant with wrinkled seeds?
options: Tall plant with blended (partly wrinkled) seeds
questions.explanation: Same as above: F₂ from dihybrid cross produces all four phenotypic combinations (tall-round, tall-wrinkled, short-round, short-wrinkled). Medium-height and blended seeds are not possible due to complete dominance.

questions.question: A section of DNA providing information for one protein is called—
questions.explanation: A gene is a segment of DNA that codes for a specific protein or functional RNA. Nucleus contains genes, chromosomes are structures carrying genes, and traits are expressed characteristics.

questions.question: Amongst which of the following animals is the sex of the offsprings not genetically determined?
questions.explanation: In some snails, sex can be determined by environmental factors or age (they can change sex). Humans, birds, and dogs have genetic sex determination (XY in humans/dogs, ZW in birds).

questions.question: What is the probability that a human progeny will be a boy?
questions.explanation: In humans, sex is determined by the sperm carrying either X or Y chromosome. Both are equally likely, so probability of a boy (XY) is approximately 50% (slightly varies due to secondary sex ratio but biologically 50%).

questions.question: Select all that apply: There is an inbuilt tendency of variation during reproduction because of:
questions.explanation: Variation arises from mutations (errors in DNA copying), crossing over during meiosis, and independent assortment of chromosomes. Asexual reproduction produces clones with minimal variation (only mutations).

questions.question: Which of the following traits is an example of an acquired characteristic that cannot be passed to the next generation through biological inheritance?
options: A surgical scar on the abdominal wall
The natural pigment level of the skin
The structural shape of the earlobes
questions.explanation: Acquired characteristics (like scars, tattoos, or lost limbs) are not inherited because they do not alter the DNA in gametes. Insulin production, skin pigment, and earlobe shape are genetically determined traits.

questions.question: If the genotypes determining the blood groups of a couple are IᴬIᴼ and IᴬIᴮ, then the probability of their first child having type O blood is:
questions.explanation: Parent 1: IᴬIᴼ can produce gametes Iᴬ or Iᴼ. Parent 2: IᴬIᴮ can produce gametes Iᴬ or Iᴮ. To have type O blood (IᴼIᴼ), the child must inherit Iᴼ from both parents. Parent 2 has no Iᴼ allele, so probability = 0.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered advanced genetics and probability calculations! 🧬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review blood type inheritance and acquired vs inherited traits.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on impossible F₁ outcomes and sex determination.
scoreMessages.0: Please review polymorphism, genetic variation mechanisms, and Mendelian conclusions.
